title: The Riddle of the Small Letter
author: JustPlayBo

intro.title: The Riddle of the Small Letter
intro.html:
London, a drizzling afternoon. As Watson skims the Times beside the Baker Street fire, a Baker Street Irregular knocks at the door.

“Mr Holmes, Mrs Carruthers sends us. Her husband has not come home in two days, and his private library has been ransacked.”

The investigation begins at the Criterion Club, the last place Mr Carruthers was seen.

Hint: whenever the narration invites you to “circle a letter”, mark it in your notebook — some later clues will only unlock if you have collected the right letter.

solution.title: Solution
solution.html: The culprit is the butler of the Criterion Club, who had access to Mr Carruthers's private member card. The circled letters S and W tell the story: S for the Strongbox opened, W for the Wellington recovered at the Abbey.

3SO.default:
At the Criterion Club the doorman remembers Mr Carruthers: he came in on Wednesday afternoon and never left through the main entrance.

A private member card lies on the floor — it was not there yesterday.

conditional.html: With the letter S — the member card opens a strongbox in the cellar; inside, a map of the Westminster area marked in red ink.

6SO.default: St James's Palace is quiet. The guards have noticed nothing unusual in the past 48 hours.

8SO.default: At the Diogenes Club silence is the rule. Mycroft receives you with a nod: “Brother of mine, if you seek a missing man, try circling the letter W.”

conditional.html: With S and W — Mycroft, without raising his eyes: “Westminster Abbey, Wellington pavilion. Your inquiry ends there.”

17SO.default: Westminster Abbey welcomes a few tourists. The custodian points to the chapel of St Edward.

conditional.html: With the letter W — in the pavilion dedicated to Wellington you find recent traces of forced entry and a scrap of dark blue cloth, the same as Mr Carruthers's coat.
With S and W — behind Wellington's tomb, a hideout contains a diary: Mr Carruthers was held there. Who led him here remains to be uncovered.

items.text: Who is responsible for Mr Carruthers's disappearance?
items.answer: The butler of the Criterion Club.
In the hideout behind Wellington's tomb at Westminster Abbey.

items.text: Which item recovered at the Criterion Club proved decisive for the inquiry?
items.answer: The private member card dropped on the Club floor.
items.rationale: The card opened a strongbox containing the area map.

items.text: What allowed Mycroft to point to Westminster?
items.answer: The combination of the S (strongbox) and W (Wellington) clues.

items.text: What colour is the scrap of cloth found at the Abbey?
On which day of the week did Carruthers enter the Criterion?
Who knocked on the Baker Street door at the start of the case?
items.answer: A Baker Street Irregular, on behalf of Mrs Carruthers.

items.text: Which chapel does the Abbey custodian first point to?
items.answer: The chapel of St Edward (a misdirection).

steps.title: Criterion Club
steps.description: Speak to the doorman, spot the member card on the floor, and inspect the strongbox in the cellar.

steps.title: Diogenes Club
steps.description: Mycroft confirms the connection with Westminster; he hints that you should circle the W.

steps.title: Westminster Abbey
steps.description: Find the hideout behind Wellington's tomb and Carruthers's diary.
